_changed: 2026-07-09: roundtrip_ship_insured (scalar, per-card) replaced by shipping.bands (per-order, keyed by declared value and card count). The old field lied: PSA and BGS both bill shipping per shipment, not per card, and both scale with declared value.
_rule: No number enters this file without a verified_on date. Anything unverified stays null and triggers REFUSED.

psa._max_declared_value_note: [verify] Regular tier's declared-value ceiling not captured; the wizard accepted $250 without complaint.

turnaround._note: [verify] Sources conflict: PSA Regular reported anywhere from 40-60 to ~70 calendar days in 2026. Do not assert. Pull the live estimate from the submission wizard at submit time.

shipping._inbound_note: PSA sells the inbound FedEx label; the charge posts after package drop-off, not at checkout. Shipping outbound yourself (USPS or FedEx only, never UPS/DHL) may beat this and is an open optimization.

bands.source: PSA Online Submission Center, Submission Summary screen, Regular tier, 1 item, $250 declared value

bgs._max_declared_value_note: [verify] Beckett publishes no declared-value caps on these service levels.

turnaround._note: Business days while the card is in Beckett's possession. Transit both ways is on top. 45 business days is roughly 9 calendar weeks.

shipping._inbound_note: [confirm/fill] Beckett does not sell an inbound label in this flow; you ship to Dallas yourself. Outbound insured cost is NOT yet captured and must be added before any BGS-vs-PSA comparison is honest.

bands.source: Beckett submission wizard, Order Summary, Standard With Subgrades, 1 card
bands._note: Insurance and return shipping were identical at the Base tier, so these bands key off declared value, not service level. Declared value assumed $250 to match the PSA quote — [verify] that the Beckett flow took the same figure.

bgs_base._subgrade_upcharge_note: $3 upcharge for any 10s where subgrades are added.

turnaround._note: Listed as '75+'. The plus is load-bearing: treat as a floor, not an estimate. ~15 calendar weeks minimum, plus transit.

bands.source: Beckett submission wizard, Order Summary, Base With Subgrades, 1 card

graded_price_multiplier_note: Grading fee is not the deciding cost. The same physical card nets differently by slab: PSA commands the premium in vintage and Pokemon; BGS grades harder, so a BGS 9.5 often reflects what PSA would call a 10. Model this as DIFFERENT graded-price nodes per service in the per-card file, never as one price with a swapped fee. [verify] per card, per service, with dated sold comps.

marketplace_fees._note: [verify] Not yet captured. Enter current, dated values before any verdict is trusted.
